title: dactylorhiza traunsteineri var. blyttii
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteineri var. blyttii, also known as the Blytt's Marsh Orchid, is a rare and endangered species of terrestrial orchid native to Europe. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues and lower blood pressure due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza traunsteineri var. blyttii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying and processing its aerial parts, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
